title: asperula tinctoria subsp. hungarorum
intro: Asperula tinctoria subsp. hungarorum, also known as the Hungarian woodruff, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Asperula tinctoria subsp. hungarorum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Asperula tinctoria subsp. hungarorum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
